behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff (evaluate-stage writer -> artifact store/selector), not single-function logic. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP do not apply from behavior text. Rejected Agentic because behavior only specifies destination artifact keying ("stores grounding into ... artifact"), not model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage command through real in-process artifact wiring, then assert `grounding` is written and `plan` is untouched by this step.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test exercises cross-module write path; a pure unit test of key constants would miss miswired artifact routing.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is state handoff plus write routing across implement flow components. No required real OS/network/subprocess. Rejected Agentic: text focuses on read/write behavior ("reads grounding ... writes ... to plan"), not judging model content quality.
behaviors.test_description: Seed run-context artifacts with `grounding`, execute implement flow with deterministic model/tool stubs, assert input pull from `grounding` path and output persisted to `plan`.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires wired flow and artifact layer; lower-level unit tests would not catch contract mismatch between reader and writer modules.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when deep-dive stage interacts with existing issue-derived state and artifact writer side effects. No explicit real infra dependency. Rejected Agentic: disqualifying text is about side-effect suppression ("no longer overwrites `plan`") given pre-existing diagnosis, not quality of model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content containing diagnosis, run deep-dive stage through normal in-process pipeline, assert no write/update occurs on `plan` artifact.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs stage + artifact-store wiring to expose unintended writes; isolated function tests can miss side-effect paths.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is lineage/state propagation across modules in one run context (producer metadata -> resolver -> consumer). No required external OS/network process in behavior text. Rejected Workflow because only one downstream consumption check is required, not validating full multi-agent sequence outcomes.
behaviors.test_description: Execute evaluate-stage write of `grounding` into a shared run context, then invoke implement-stage resolver and assert it retrieves that exact prior artifact lineage.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires integrated context store and lineage resolver; unit mocks risk hiding key/namespace mismatch.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification:
Behavior explicitly requires end-to-end multi-step chain with earlier-stage grounding influencing final-stage plan output. This is MULTI-STEP with agent decisions across stages. Rejected Integration because behavior text demands "full multi-step agent run" and outcome reflection in final plan, which can be masked by deterministic stubs.
MEMORY-SLOTS-HYBRID evidence: explicit 3-stage sequence and cross-stage influence requirement.
MEMORY-SLOTS-HYBRID boundary signal: correctness depends on sequential agent-stage handoff, not a single interface.
MEMORY-SLOTS-HYBRID adjacent-lower miss-proof: integration stubs can pass routing while missing whether final plan actually incorporates grounding semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run real chain `deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement` in one run context and assert final `plan` content materially incorporates grounding generated earlier (with rubric-based assertion over multiple runs).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test description requires full staged pipeline and agent outputs; lower levels cannot validate cross-step behavioral influence.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: This is fallback-path orchestration over shared artifact state (missing optional input + safe write behavior). No required real OS/network/subprocess. Rejected Agentic: disqualifying text centers on safety/valid artifact emission under missing grounding, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Start with run context lacking `grounding` but containing other artifacts, execute implement flow, assert `plan` is produced and pre-existing unrelated artifacts remain unchanged.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs integrated artifact store/state checks; unit tests of fallback branch alone may miss corruption in shared storage wiring.
